sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 20.15 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.20)

statement.latex: Let$$be an infinite cardinal. If a residually finite group$G$is embeddable in the full permutation group of a set of cardinality$$, must it be embeddable in the direct product of$$finite groups? See (G.\,M.\,Bergman, , 18 (2007), 349--403). The converse is true: any such direct product is residually finite and embeddable in the indicated permutation group.
